mnemonics: Hash STATIC: Same Table Always, Totally Inflexible Capacity
keyPoints: Static hashing uses a fixed number of buckets, determined at creation time.
It is efficient for databases with a known, stable number of records.
Common implementations include hash tables with linear probing or chaining for collision resolution.
commonMistakes: Assuming static hashing can efficiently handle a significant increase in data beyond its initial capacity.
Forgetting that a poor hash function can lead to clustering and severe performance degradation, even in a static setup.
